title: The Windy Day
summary: Two children in Edinburgh battle a very windy walk to school and laugh about the strong gusts.

body.speaker: Ella
body.text: Jack! Hold your hat! The wind is so strong today!

body.speaker: Jack
body.text: I know! My hat almost flew away!

body.speaker: Ella
body.text: Look at the trees. The big one is bending.

body.speaker: Jack
body.text: Wow, look at those leaves! They are dancing in the air.

body.speaker: Ella
body.text: My scarf is everywhere. I cannot keep it on.

body.speaker: Jack
body.text: Walk close to the wall. The wind is softer there.

body.speaker: Ella
body.text: Good idea. Oh! That man's umbrella turned inside out!

body.speaker: Jack
body.text: Ha ha! Now it looks like a big black flower.

body.speaker: Ella
body.text: Hold my hand. I do not want to fall.

body.speaker: Jack
body.text: Okay. We can walk together. The school is just there.

body.speaker: Ella
body.text: Can you hear the wind? It sings in my ears.

body.speaker: Jack
body.text: Yes! Like a very long whistle.

body.speaker: Ella
body.text: Look, a paper is flying high above the houses!

body.speaker: Jack
body.text: Maybe it is somebody's homework. The teacher will be sad.

body.speaker: Ella
body.text: Quick, the gate! We are almost there.

body.speaker: Jack
body.text: Inside at last! My cheeks are pink and my hair is a mess.

annotations.exampleEn: It is windy! Hold your hat!
The tall tree is bending in the wind.
The yellow leaves were dancing in the air.
Wear a scarf in cold weather.
My umbrella turned inside out in the wind.
Hold my hand at the road.
The kettle made a long whistle.
I finished my homework last night.
After the wind, my hair was a mess.

grammarNotes.title: Present continuous to describe a scene
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả điều đang xảy ra trước mắt, dùng 'is/are + V-ing': 'The big tree is bending.' / 'They are dancing in the air.' / 'A paper is flying high.' Hai bé dùng cấu trúc này suốt cả đoạn để 'tường thuật trực tiếp' gió bão.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use present continuous 'is/are + V-ing' to describe what is happening: 'The big tree is bending.' / 'They are dancing in the air.' / 'A paper is flying high.' The children use it like a live report of the storm.

grammarNotes.title: Imperatives and friendly suggestions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Hai bé liên tục đưa ra gợi ý/mệnh lệnh nhẹ: 'Hold your hat!' / 'Walk close to the wall.' / 'Hold my hand.' Đây là tiếng Anh thực tế khi bạn bè giúp nhau trong tình huống thực.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The children constantly suggest small actions: 'Hold your hat!' / 'Walk close to the wall.' / 'Hold my hand.' This is real-life A1 English for friends helping each other.

translationVi: Ella: Jack! Giữ mũ đi! Hôm nay gió mạnh quá! Jack: Tớ biết! Mũ của tớ suýt bay mất! Ella: Nhìn mấy cái cây kìa. Cái cây to đang cong xuống. Jack: Ồ, nhìn mấy chiếc lá kìa! Chúng đang nhảy múa trong không khí. Ella: Khăn của tớ bay tứ tung. Tớ không giữ nổi. Jack: Đi sát vào tường ấy. Gió ở đó nhẹ hơn. Ella: Ý hay. Ôi! Cái ô của bác kia bị lộn ngược ra ngoài kìa! Jack: Ha ha! Giờ trông như một bông hoa đen to vậy. Ella: Nắm tay tớ với. Tớ sợ ngã. Jack: Ừ. Mình đi cùng nhau. Trường ngay đằng kia rồi. Ella: Cậu có nghe thấy gió không? Nó kêu vù vù bên tai tớ. Jack: Có chứ! Giống một tiếng huýt sáo dài. Ella: Nhìn kìa, có tờ giấy đang bay cao trên những mái nhà! Jack: Có khi là bài tập của ai đó. Cô giáo sẽ buồn lắm. Ella: Nhanh nào, tới cổng rồi! Sắp tới nơi rồi. Jack: Cuối cùng cũng vào trong rồi! Hai má tớ đỏ và tóc tớ rối hết cả lên.

questions.prompt: What is Jack's problem at the start?
What is the big tree doing?
What does Jack suggest to do?
questions.explanation: 'Walk close to the wall. The wind is softer there.'

questions.prompt: What happens to a man's umbrella?
What does Ella ask Jack to do?
questions.explanation: 'Hold my hand. I do not want to fall.'

questions.prompt: What does the wind sound like?
What flies high above the houses?
questions.explanation: 'a paper is flying high above the houses!'

questions.prompt: What does Jack guess about the paper?
How does Jack look when he gets inside?
options: Cheeks pink and hair a mess
questions.explanation: 'My cheeks are pink and my hair is a mess.'

questions.prompt: How do the children feel about the wind?
options: They struggle with it but also laugh
questions.explanation: They laugh about the umbrella and joke about the paper, even while holding on.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Sometimes 'bad' weather can also be fun. Tell about a windy or rainy day you remember. What did you do?
